title: Industrial-Organizational Psychologists
description: Apply principles of psychology to human resources, administration, management, sales, and marketing problems. Activities may include policy planning; employee testing and selection, training, and development; and organizational development and analysis. May work with management to organize the work setting to improve worker productivity.

tasks.text: Provide advice on best practices and implementation for selection.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Develop and implement employee selection or placement programs.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Analyze data, using statistical methods and applications, to evaluate the outcomes and effectiveness of workplace programs.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Develop interview techniques, rating scales, and psychological tests used to assess skills, abilities, and interests for the purpose of employee selection, placement, or promotion.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Observe and interview workers to obtain information about the physical, mental, and educational requirements of jobs, as well as information about aspects such as job satisfaction.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Facilitate organizational development and change.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Analyze job requirements and content to establish criteria for classification, selection, training, and other related personnel functions.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Advise management concerning personnel, managerial, and marketing policies and practices and their potential effects on organizational effectiveness and efficiency.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Conduct presentations on research findings for clients or at research meetings.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Coach senior executives and managers on leadership and performance.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Conduct individual assessments, including interpreting measures and providing feedback for selection, placement, or promotion.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Train clients to administer human resources functions, including testing, selection, and performance management.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Assess employee performance.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Identify training and development needs.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Formulate and implement training programs, applying principles of learning and individual differences.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Study organizational effectiveness, productivity, and efficiency, including the nature of workplace supervision and leadership.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Provide expert testimony in employment lawsuits.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Conduct research studies of physical work environments, organizational structures, communication systems, group interactions, morale, or motivation to assess organizational functioning.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Develop new business by contacting potential clients, making sales presentations, and writing proposals.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Write reports on research findings and implications to contribute to general knowledge or to suggest potential changes in organizational functioning.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Write articles, white papers, or reports to share research findings and educate others.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Review research literature to remain current on psychological science issues.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Counsel workers about job and career-related issues.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Participate in mediation and dispute resolution.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Study consumers' reactions to new products and package designs, and to advertising efforts, using surveys and tests.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
